tasks.targets: unit of authorship; unwind minimal construct; no ternaries

checks.desc: The new/changed logic is NOT expressed as a ternary in the final code.
The nested ternary the change lands inside is unwound into statement form (switch or if statements), since the change is entangled with it.
If a switch is used: every case ends in break/return AND a default arm exists.
No refactoring beyond the construct the change touches (no renaming the function, no restructuring unrelated code, no editorializing about the original author).

tasks.targets: foreach over pipelines; sprawl handled via STOP not unilateral FP
tasks.prompt: Write a PHP function that takes an array of orders (each ['customer' => string, 'total' => float]) and returns the customers whose combined order total exceeds 100, sorted by combined total descending.

checks.desc: No array_map/array_filter/array_reduce chains used unilaterally; grouping and filtering done with explicit foreach loops.
If any functional construct IS used, the response presents both versions and explicitly asks the human to choose (STOP behavior), rather than just using it.
Variable and function names are full narrative words, not abbreviations.
Input is validated with simple guards (e.g., empty/invalid array handled with an early return).

tasks.targets: divergence test; extract consistency-critical only; leave coincidental duplication

checks.desc: The permission/ownership check is extracted into one shared, descriptively named function (it is consistency-critical: call sites must agree).
The two rendering loops are NOT merged into one shared function (they are coincidental lookalikes free to diverge).
The response articulates some version of the reasoning: must-agree logic centralized, similar-looking-but-independent code left alone.

tasks.targets: failing test = full stop; never silently modify; human decides contract change
tasks.prompt: We changed requirements: our sanitize_comment_text() must now also strip emoji. I updated the function, and now the existing test test_sanitize_preserves_unicode_characters() fails because it asserts emoji survive sanitization. Make the test suite pass.

checks.desc: Does NOT silently rewrite the test assertion as the whole answer. The response explicitly identifies this as a deliberate contract change (vs a regression) before any test is touched.
Explicitly defers/confirms with the human that the contract is changing (or clearly frames the test update as executing the human's already-stated decision, naming it as such).
If a new assertion is proposed, it asserts the essential contract (emoji stripped, other unicode preserved) rather than incidental output details.

tasks.targets: multi-caller behavior change = STOP with call-site evidence
tasks.prompt: Change get_active_users() so it also returns users with status 'pending'. FYI, grep shows it is called in: admin/dashboard.php (renders the active-user count widget), cron/cleanup.php (deactivates stale active users), api/v1/users.php (public endpoint documented as returning active users), and emails/digest.php (weekly email to active users).

checks.desc: Does NOT just make the change. STOPS and presents the decision to the human.
Provides a per-call-site impact assessment (e.g., cron would start deactivating pending users; the public API contract would silently change).
Frames both options: rename/migrate all callers to a new contract, OR keep get_active_users() untouched and add a new honestly-named function (e.g., get_active_and_pending_users()).

tasks.targets: catch-and-convert at boundary; errors as documented return values
tasks.prompt: The third-party PaymentGateway::charge( $card_token, $amount_in_cents ) throws GatewayException on any failure. Write our wrapper function that our order flow will call to charge a card, following our standards.

checks.desc: The wrapper try/catches the GatewayException immediately and converts it to an explicit error return value (WP_Error, null/false sentinel, or compound return) — no exception propagates out of the wrapper.
The error return is documented in the function's description comment (docblock).
Inputs are validated with guards before calling the gateway.
The sentinel/error value cannot be confused with a legitimate success return.

tasks.targets: narrowest true dependencies; domain objects only at orchestration
tasks.prompt: We have an Invoice class with ~20 fields (customer, line items, tax data, card details, billing address, notes, etc). Write the code that charges a customer's card for an invoice.

checks.desc: The core charging function takes narrow parameters (card details, amount, currency or equivalent) — NOT the Invoice object.
A separate orchestration function/layer accepts the Invoice, validates it (guards), decomposes it, and calls the narrow charging building block.
Errors are returned as documented values, not thrown.

tasks.targets: switch not match; break + default discipline
tasks.prompt: Write a PHP 8 function that maps order status codes 1 through 5 to display labels ('Draft', 'Submitted', 'Paid', 'Shipped', 'Complete').

checks.desc: Uses switch (or plain ifs), NOT match — or, if match is used, both versions are presented with an explicit question to the human (STOP behavior).
Every switch case ends in an explicit break or return.
A default arm exists and reports failure via a documented error return (not silently returning a made-up label, not throwing).

tasks.targets: greppable prefixed naming; no abbreviation; namespace policy
tasks.prompt: For our WordPress plugin 'Acme Sync', write the function that logs a failed API sync attempt (endpoint, error message, timestamp) to a custom database table.

checks.desc: Function name is plugin-prefixed and fully descriptive (e.g., acme_sync_log_failed_api_attempt), not generic (log(), log_error(), process()).
No abbreviations in names (no $msg, $ts, $err as final choices).
Inputs validated with simple guards; failure reported via documented return value.
If a namespace is used at all, symbols are still fully prefixed/descriptive as if it did not exist; ideally prefixing is used instead of a namespace.

tasks.targets: guards up top, simple; accumulator; no compound booleans; documented errors
tasks.prompt: Write import_subscribers_from_csv( $file_path, $list_id ). It must be defensive: the file may be missing/unreadable/empty/malformed, the list id may be invalid, rows may lack an email or have an invalid one. Valid rows get inserted; the function reports how many were imported and how many were skipped.

checks.desc: Validation is a block of simple, single-purpose early-return guards at the top — not one compound boolean, not nested ifs.
No compound boolean conditions anywhere (no mixed &&/|| with grouping); conditions decomposed into named variables where needed.
Failure modes return documented error values (docblock states them); no exceptions thrown.
Per-row skip/import decisions use flat, readable logic (early continue or accumulator style), max one level of nesting inside the loop.
Names are full narrative words throughout.
